reasoning: GEOMETRY CHECK: (1) Macro-feature count at 1x: ~5-7 distinct lighter-green grass-tuft clusters plus 2-3 warm orange/brown dot accents scattered across the 32x32 tile — count is within the 3-8 acceptable range. (2) Largest feature scale: light-green highlight clusters span roughly 3-5px each; the warm accent dots are ~2-3px — the lower end of the 4-8px target, approaching speckle territory but still individually readable at 1x. (3) Versus a 32x32 player sprite: tile energy is clearly quieter — medium forest-green base with subdued variation; no single element dominates the frame; would not compete with sprites placed on top. Overall the tile passes density intent (not featureless, not chaotic mush) and reads as ground, but the feature scale skews slightly small/fine rather than bold grass tufts, keeping it in marginal rather than a clean pass.
